[vc_row][vc_column][vc_empty_space height=”50px” el_class=”height30″][/vc_column][/vc_row][vc_row el_class=”flexrow”][vc_column width=”1/4″ el_class=”flextwo gt-custom-sidebar-column”][vc_widget_sidebar sidebar_id=”honeymoon-package-international-sidebar”][/vc_column][vc_column width=”3/4″ el_class=”second-div-text flexone”][vc_custom_heading text=”Affordable Phuket Honeymoon Packages from ATW Travels”][vc_column_text]

A honeymoon is a great time to relax and unwind after the stress of wedding planning. It is also an excellent way to reflect on your relationship and pave the way for the future. We’ve helped thousands of couples plan a memorable honeymoon at a price they can afford. If you want your first romantic vacation with your partner to be extra special, visit ATW Travels.

[/vc_column_text][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Is Phuket an Ideal Place for A Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket is the largest mountain island in Thailand. It is home to magnificent beaches, stunning seaside resorts, world-class spas, and delectable restaurants offering heavenly spicy Thai cuisine. Whether you plan on visiting the many Buddhist temples, enjoying some snorkeling, or basking in the sun while lying on the beach, there’s something for everyone at Phuket. At ATW Travels, we offer affordable Phuket honeymoon packages that showcase its rich culture and natural sights. They’re the perfect bundle of romance and happiness for newly married couples.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19467″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Tips To Remember When Choosing Phuket Honeymoon Packages?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket is a perfect destination for honeymooners because of its diverse beaches and vibrant nightlife. But if you’re keen on making your vacation special, here are a few things to remember before booking a romantic Phuket honeymoon tour package:

  • Phuket is undoubtedly beautiful. But like any tropical island, it is terribly humid. The best time to visit the island is between December to March when the weather is comfortable, and humidity is bearable. Remember to keep a water bottle and pack light, viz. some shorts, light pants, t-shirts, etc.
  • Mosquitoes are everywhere in Phuket, including 5-star hotels. Most accommodations provide complimentary insect repellants – make sure you carry when with you while going out for sightseeing. Or, you can buy one as they’re readily available at all local shops.
  • Locals in Phuket speak passable English. They can answer basic questions, but not more. It’s better to ask your tour guide to help you while shopping. Remember to download a translation app on your phone for basic Thai words if you’re in an emergency.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”How Much Does a Phuket Honeymoon Package Cost?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket is a little expensive when compared to other tourist destinations in Thailand. But you can always find affordable Phuket honeymoon packages if you’re alright with accommodations.

However, the cost depends on the number of days you plan to stay, accommodation chosen, sightseeing activities offered, and other add-on services preferred.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner full_width=”stretch_row_content_no_spaces” css=”.vc_custom_1645675083319{margin-bottom: 20px !important;padding-top: 40px !important;padding-bottom: 40px !important;background-color: #1a2b48 !important;}” el_id=”mobileshoecontact” el_class=”show-mobile”][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1646219800587{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

info@atwtravels.com

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][vc_column_inner el_class=”whitecontent” width=”1/2″ css=”.vc_custom_1644654313324{border-left-width: 1px !important;border-left-color: #ffffff !important;border-left-style: solid !important;}”][vc_column_text css=”.vc_custom_1653551532249{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

Book Your Dream Vacay Today!

[/vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Which Part of Phuket Is Best for Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket has unique beaches speckled all around the island with rocking and vibrant nightlife. There are plenty of bars, nightclubs, and fine dining restaurants specializing in Thai food for honeymooners to enjoy their stay. Here are some of the popular places that are often included in most Phuket honeymoon packages:

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Patong” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

This place is famous for its epic nightlife and white sand beaches, perfect for relaxing and basking in the sun. You could also enjoy some of the favourite street foods in Patong or sign up for jet skiing, parasailing, kayaking, and other water sports activities.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19468″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Kata” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Located on the island’s west coast, Kata Beach is one of the trendiest places in Phuket. It is less crowded than Patong and hence popular among honeymooners. The entire beach is 1.5 km long and famous for its golden sands, beautiful weather, and water sports activities.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Karon” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Located on the island’s west coast, Karon is one of the longest beaches in Phuket. People love this place for its clear turquoise waters and powdery beaches. Compared to Patong, Karon is quieter – if you’re looking for some privacy, this is the beach to visit.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What Is the Best Time to Visit Phuket for Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Summers in Phuket are scorching and humid – this isn’t the best time to visit, especially for a honeymoon. We suggest visiting Phuket between December and March if you don’t want to feel irritated and sticky on your vacation. The island experiences monsoon between May to October, which isn’t really an excellent time to visit because of the humidity and unpredictable rain. But it’s the best time to book some of the cheapest Phuket honeymoon packages.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Why Choose ATW Travels as Your Travel Partner?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

At ATW Travels, we have been curating exclusive honeymoon packages for over four decades. Over the years, we have helped create lifetime memories for thousands of newlyweds across the country. Our team can customize a romantic Phuket honeymoon tour package that matches your tastes and budget. We’ll cover every little aspect you’ll need for the vacation, including accommodation, hotel transfer, sightseeing, and adventure activities.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19469″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What Are Some Beaches to Explore on A Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket has some of the most extraordinary beaches in Thailand. While the popular ones are crowded, plenty of quaint beaches are perfect for a romantic getaway.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Ao Sane” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Located on the southwest coast of Phuket in the north of Nai Harn beach, Ao Sane is far away from the hustle and bustle of noisy hawkers and tourists. You could rent beach chairs to bask in the sun, hire snorkelling gear, and undertake diving lessons.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Banana Beach” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

This beach gets its name from the multiple banana boats that ferry Rawai Beach and Chalong Pier passengers. It is an excellent place for swimming and snorkelling. You could also enjoy some local Thai cuisine at the only restaurant near the beach.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Freedom Beach” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

If you wish to witness marine life in its true realm, this is a must spot on your list. Located southwest of Patong beach, Freedom Beach is covered by thick jungles and granite rocks.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What Are Some of The Best Places to Visit in Phuket for Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Phuket has much more than just stunning views and magnificent beaches. There are many other things and activities that you can do here with your partner to enhance your overall honeymoon experience.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Island Tours” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

If you are a movie buff and would love to explore places where famous movie scenes were shot, take a Phuket Island Tour, an activity that includes visits to popular locations. This includes visiting the James Bond rock and the Maya Bay where Leonardo DiCaprio movie The Beach was shot.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_single_image image=”19470″ img_size=”full” alignment=”center”][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Phuket Big Buddha” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

The Grand Buddha of Phuket is a 45-metre statue of the Buddha built atop the Khao Nakkerd Hill and is one of Phuket’s most famous landmarks. It is also the best place for a panoramic view of Phuket Island.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Rawai Village” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

If you want to experience the authentic side of Phuket, venture to this small village and witness the lifestyle of Moken, a tribe of ‘sea gypsies.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Wat Chalong” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

Out of all the 29 temples, it is unquestionably one of the most beautiful temples in Phuket.

Some of the other places that must be included in your Phuket honeymoon packages include Gibbon Rehabilitation Centre, Phuket Trickeye Museum, Sirinat National Park, and of course, the endless breath-taking beaches.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”What Are Some of The Best Hotels and Resorts to Stay in Phuket for Honeymoon?” font_container=”tag:h3|text_align:left”][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Naka Island” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

This island resort is ideal for couples looking for peace and quiet. The villas on the island are spectacular, with private pools, a sea-view experience, a steam room, a sauna, and an ice room.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Sri Panwa Phuket Luxury Pool Villa Hotel” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

This six-star resort offers a beautiful view of the ocean and is the ultimate in style and comfort. It is the perfect love nest for couples looking to unwind and enjoy.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_custom_heading text=”Six Senses Yao Noi” font_container=”tag:h4|text_align:left”][vc_column_text]

This beautiful island resort rises from the waters of Phang Nga Bay, creating a dreamlike setting for couples. A private-infinity pool with unique spa experiences makes it a perfect place for honeymooners.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

Phuket is exceptionally beautiful and ideal for youngsters looking for the perfect combination of laidback fun and vibrant nightlife. Our Phuket honeymoon packages offer the best of this place at an affordable price.

Get to know about our daily offers by visiting our Instagram Profile.

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_row_inner][vc_column_inner][vc_column_text]

[/vc_column_text][/vc_column_inner][/vc_row_inner][vc_empty_space height=”20px” css=”.vc_custom_1646651406162{margin-bottom: 0px !important;}”]

FAQs

What Is the Best Time to Honeymoon in Phuket?
If you don’t mind the humidity or the hot weather, consider booking a honeymoon package during the summer. But the best and the busiest season is between December and March, when the weather is pleasant and ideal for swimming and boating.
How Many Days Are Enough for Phuket Trip?
You’ll need at least 4 -5 days to explore some of the best beaches in and around Phuket.
Is Phuket Safe for Honeymoon Couples?
Phuket is a relatively safe place for honeymoon couples. But make sure you contact your local tour guide while shopping or for other assistance to avoid being duped.
How Can I Reach Phuket?
The Phuket International Airport is one of the busiest airports in Thailand, welcoming millions of tourists from across the world.
How Can I Travel Within Phuket?
Despite not having the best public transportation system, Phuket is famous for its Tuk-Tuks. They are everywhere and can take you anywhere at any time of the day or night. There are also taxis and buses available, and you can even rent a motorbike which is both cheap and comfortable.
Visa Requirements for International Traveller’s Visiting Phuket
Indians can apply for a visa on arrival if they don’t intend to stay in Thailand for over 60 days. You must have a valid passport, prior hotel booking receipts, sufficient funds, and a confirmed return ticket to apply for a visa on arrival.
